metadata.notes:
agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: gap:wait_turn

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: gap:wait_turn

## Reasoning

Like its claudecode/codex siblings, this cell is really an irrlicht-dispatcher test filed under the mistral-vibe column. The scenario co-launches a `vibe` process and a DIFFERENT agent (here claudecode) in the SAME cwd and asserts irrlicht reports two rows under their correct adapter labels with no cross-contamination. There is no vibe-specific limitation: vibe is a normal foreground coding agent that contributes exactly one normal session, and its transport is fully disjoint from the partner's.

irrlicht isolates adapters at three layers, all exercised here:
1. **Process matching** — each adapter declares its own matcher. vibe uses a `CommandPattern` on `(^|/)vibe( |$)|mistral-vibe/bin/python` (agent.go / adapter.go), disjoint from claudecode's `ExactName{Name: "claude"}`. The scanner runs each matcher independently and tags each PID with its originating adapter name.
2. **Transcript roots** — adapters declare disjoint `FilesUnderRoot.Dir` paths: vibe watches `~/.vibe/logs/session/` (sessionsDir() in adapter.go), claudecode watches `~/.claude/projects/`. They never collide, so a JSONL line under one root can never reach the other adapter's parser.
3. **Per-session metrics** — `Parsers` / `PIDDiscoverers` / `SubagentCounters` in maps.go are keyed by adapter Identity.Name; metrics, subagent counts, and parent linkage all key off the (adapter, session_id) tuple. No global per-cwd aggregation exists in the domain, so nothing can leak between vibe and claudecode.

Spec bullets map cleanly: correct adapter label (recorded at presession/scan time from the matcher-tagged result), independent state transitions (two tailers, different goroutines, different files, per-session classifier), and no cross-contamination (per-adapter counters, disjoint roots). daemon=full.

## Driver: gap:wait_turn

The recipe drives ONLY the vibe half (send -> wait_turn -> sleep); the second-agent (claudecode) launch is the rig's job — run-cell-multi.sh reads `partner_adapter` off this recipe and launches BOTH interactive drivers concurrently against one shared cwd under a single --record daemon. So there is NO second-agent-launch driver gap. However, the vibe interactive driver is freshly SCAFFOLDED: DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash sleep", and `wait_turn` is a `not_implemented` stub (SEAM 2, driver-interactive.sh L168-170). The recipe needs `wait_turn`, so the semantic recipe-lint flags a gap -> driver=gap:wait_turn, consistent with every other mistral-vibe cell (e.g. 2-1_basic-turn). record ports SEAMs 1/2/3 (REPL launch, turn-done poll, transcript resolution) before it can drive.

## What irrlicht does NOT see (and why that's OK)

irrlicht has no first-class 'workspace' row; the session list is flat and the dashboard groups by cwd visually. That is correct for this scenario, which asserts only per-session correctness, not aggregate workspace state.

## Recording plan

Recorded by run-cell-multi.sh in isolated coexist mode (its own --record daemon on an alternate port + IRRLICHT_HOME, so production keeps running). It launches the mistral-vibe and claudecode interactive drivers concurrently against ONE shared cwd (IRRLICHT_ONBOARD_CWD). The daemon observes vibe via its `~/.vibe/logs/session` fswatcher and claudecode via `~/.claude/projects` — both keyed off the real $HOME — labeling each adapter independently. This cell stores the vibe transcript for the replay golden; its events.jsonl + expected.jsonl span the whole workspace (both adapters) and are adapter-agnostic (agentA = first to working, agentB = the other).
caveats: The recipe runs two different agent CLIs concurrently, so run-cell-multi.sh needs both `vibe` and `claude` installed + authenticated. ONE recording satisfies both matrix rows: the mistral-vibe and claudecode cells share the workspace events.jsonl + expected.jsonl, differing only in which transcript each stores for its replay golden.

Whether vibe emits a transient proc-<PID> presession row born ready before the real session_<...> birth is not confirmable from source (single-birth adapter; sessionIDFromPath). The shared spec anchors every phase on a WORKING transition specifically to skip any born-ready placeholders, so it holds either way — record settles the emission detail.

cost is not observable for mistral-vibe (parser never reads stats.session_cost; the model is absent from core/pkg/capacity), but this workspace-level spec is adapter-agnostic and pins no per-adapter observation vector.

sources.note: vibe watches ~/.vibe/logs/session (sessionsDir) and matches its process via CommandPattern `(^|/)vibe( |$)|mistral-vibe/bin/python` — disjoint from claudecode's ~/.claude/projects root and ExactName{claude} matcher, so no collision.
Parsers / PIDDiscoverers / ProcessNames / SubagentCounters are all keyed by adapter Identity.Name — no shared global parser or counter that could leak state between vibe and the partner adapter.

Scaffolded driver: DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash sleep"; wait_turn() is a not_implemented stub (SEAM 2, L168-170). The recipe needs wait_turn -> semantic gap -> driver=gap:wait_turn; record ports SEAMs 1/2/3 before driving.

Reads partner_adapter off the primary recipe (L103-106) and launches both adapters' interactive drivers concurrently against one shared cwd under a single --record daemon — the second-agent launch is the rig's job, not a driver primitive.

preconditions: vibe AND claude CLIs on PATH and authenticated
irrlichd is recording — run-cell-multi.sh isolated coexist mode (spawns its own --record daemon on an alternate port with its own IRRLICHT_HOME, so production keeps running)
no other mistral-vibe/claudecode session is live in the run cwd
setup: run-cell-multi.sh creates ONE shared cwd and launches the mistral-vibe and claudecode interactive drivers concurrently, both pointed at it via IRRLICHT_ONBOARD_CWD
the isolated daemon observes vibe via its ~/.vibe/logs/session fswatcher and claudecode via ~/.claude/projects — both keyed off the real $HOME — so it labels each adapter independently
this cell stores the mistral-vibe transcript for the replay golden; its events.jsonl and expected.jsonl span the whole workspace (both adapters)

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: alpha

verify: events.jsonl contains TWO distinct session_ids from two different adapters, both keyed to the same cwd slug
each session has its own independent working->ready arc; no transition on one is attributed to the other
no cross-contamination: the daemon never merges or flaps the two adapters' rows
